mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market slack signals impending corporate earnings deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable or declining federal funds rates indicates robust consumer demand without monetary suppression, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals expanding corporate capacity utilization and margin improvement, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week specifically during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, or the signal reverses direction and predicts negative returns in greater than 60% of recovery regime observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and wage growth, which supports corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no correlation with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances when CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.4%, or the signal fails to predict negative returns during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in housing starts and industrial production indicates synchronized demand and supply chain strengthening, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in greater than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth signals weakening aggregate demand, which reduces corporate profit expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter specifically during late-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal predicts positive returns in greater than 65% of late-cycle observations.
